mentions.note: Tough. Big yield but never buy for yield alone. Structural challenges in food categories. Avoid in this environment.
Stock up 40% YTD but down 3 today. Wait for $73–75 entry; don't chase at current levels. Good company, bad recent stock performance warrants patience.
Down from $48 (June high) to low $20s despite good beat last quarter. Good company, bad stock. MVP anomaly; possible opportunity at depressed valuation. Reports next week; await clarity.
High-quality company on new lows repeatedly; story-telling gap. Potential MVP anomaly—wait for better entry before accumulating.
MVP (Most Value for Price) brand identified by 100X data. Offers bargain business model with proven value creation. Compounding growth candidate.

Diversified pipeline (30% North American crude, 20% US nat gas). 30% stake in West Coast LNG terminal entering 2027 service—fastest route to Asia (no Panama Canal). 5.3% yield. Elite Eight income + growth stock.

Best-run pipeline operator; 5.9% yield. Not primary LNG exporter but critical feed-gas, liquids, fractionation infrastructure. 'Arms dealer' to energy ecosystem. Benefits from broader North American gas expansion. Safe income + growth.
Exceptional Q3: revenue $24B (+8% YoY), EPS $5.25 vs. $4.15. FY26 guidance raised; trades 16x FY27E, 13x 2029E. Freight spin-off (June) unlocks value. Stock deserves higher, maybe appreciably higher.
Oil/gas services play. Still inexpensive versus cohort peers. Caller Bobby's choice. Buyer on energy upcycle thesis.
Caller moved from Oakley to GEV; Cramer likes GEV a 'heck of a lot more.' Renewable energy play with strong growth.
Own in charitable trust with double; hold current position. Stock vulnerable, wait for pullback before buying; could drop 10% Monday. Market too awful to add at $125–130.
High school investor Georgia's pick. Cramer blessed aggressive growth stock for youth investor. Defense/energy thesis with upside.

Cleanest, most established pure-play LNG exporter. 19.4B of 20B revenue from LNG. Two major facilities (Sabine, Corpus Christi). Trading all-time highs, +45% YTD, 20x 2026E. Entry point for cautious investors seeking direct LNG exposure.
March 'goes in like a lion' but 'comes out like a lamb'—Lam is the winner. Buy 50 shares Monday, buy another 50 on 10% dip. Semiconductor equipment play with strong upside.

Largest company in world, but P/E cheaper than Sherwin-Williams paint company. Historically fast-growing, best-run enterprise trading at discount. Long-term buy but could fall further before catalyst clarifies.
Too risky; could lose too much money in current volatile market. Avoid.
Utility selling majority infrastructure stake to KKR. Still liked for regulated utility safety and some LNG upside, but better LNG plays exist (Cheniere, Venture Global).
MVP brand; everybody loves bargain business model. Value-for-price offering drives faster growth and multiple expansion.

Speculative high-upside pure-play. IPO'd Jan 2025 at $25, crashed to $7, now $15+ (+130% YTD). Calcasieu Pass arbitration settling; CP2 LNG phase 2 financing just closed—expects largest US exporter. For true believers in LNG boom.
80% of trillion-dollar march came from multiple expansion. Added quality/convenience to price appeal. High-income shoppers increasingly active. MVP thesis play.
